title: Bard — Zero-Knowledge Seed-Escrow Recovery (ADR-0016 / Step S7)
description: FROZEN 2026-06-18 (ADR-0016 / Step S7). The MVP recovery answer to ADR-0009's open 'user-key recovery ceremony'. A device's Ed25519 identity is derived from a 256-bit seed. The CLIENT wraps that seed TWICE — once under an Argon2id key from the user's app password, once under a one-time OMG code (Crockford base32) — and uploads the two CIPHERTEXTS keyed by a lightweight account handle (email / username). The server stores ONLY ciphertext + the device public key and can NEVER decrypt the seed: the wrapping keys (password / OMG code) never reach the server. POST /recovery/escrow is authed by the device's own EdDSA token (FleetOrDeviceVerifier; sub=deviceId), binds the handle -> publicKey, and stores the two opaque wraps — 409 if the handle is already claimed by a DIFFERENT publicKey, idempotent for the SAME publicKey (overwrites wraps, supporting OMG rotation). GET /recovery/escrow/{handle} is UNAUTHENTICATED (a recovering device has no token yet) and returns the ciphertext only (useless without the password/OMG code); 404 if the handle is unknown. The server treats wraps.* as opaque base64 — it never parses, decodes, or decrypts them. This is 'hardware-backup-token'-class recovery, not server custody of keys (ADR-0016 Reconciliation (b)).

AccountHandle.description: A lightweight account handle (email / username) the escrow is keyed by, so recovery on a new device has something to locate. NOT a credential and NOT secret on its own — knowing the handle yields only undecryptable ciphertext.

PublicKey.description: The device's base64-encoded (standard alphabet, with padding) Ed25519 public key — exactly 32 bytes once decoded (RFC 8032). Bound to the handle on first escrow; the same handle escrowing again MUST present this same key (a different key is a 409). Public material by definition.

Wrap.description: An OPAQUE base64 ciphertext blob: the 256-bit identity seed wrapped under a client-held key (the Argon2id-derived app-password key, or the one-time OMG code). The server stores and round-trips this string verbatim and NEVER decodes, parses, or decrypts it — without the user's password/OMG code it is useless. Treated as sensitive even though it is undecryptable server-side.

Wraps.description: The two ciphertext wraps of the SAME seed: 'password' under the Argon2id-from-app-password key, 'omg' under the one-time OMG code. Either alone recovers the seed (with its respective key), so a user who has their password OR their OMG code can recover.

EscrowRequest.description: POST /recovery/escrow body. Authed by the device's own EdDSA token (FleetOrDeviceVerifier; sub=deviceId). Binds handle -> publicKey and stores the two opaque ciphertext wraps. The server never decrypts wraps.*.

EscrowResponse.description: POST /recovery/escrow 200: the escrow is stored (created, or overwritten idempotently for the same publicKey — OMG rotation). Carries no ciphertext back.

EscrowFetch.description: GET /recovery/escrow/{handle} 200 (UNAUTHENTICATED — a recovering device has no token yet). The device's bound public key plus the two opaque ciphertext wraps. Ciphertext only: useless without the user's app password or OMG code, which the server never holds. 404 if the handle is unknown.
